Definition: Greenhouse gas emissions attributable to a financial institution's loans and investments. Also called Scope 3 Category 15 emissions under the GHG Protocol.
Direct GHG emissions from sources owned or controlled by a company (e.g. diesel generators, company vehicles, industrial combustion).
Indirect GHG emissions from the generation of purchased electricity, heat, or steam consumed by a company.
All other indirect GHG emissions in a company's value chain, including financed emissions (Category 15) for financial institutions.
Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials. The global standard for measuring and disclosing financed emissions.
Reference: PCAF Global GHG Accounting & Reporting Standard v2

Definition: The proportion of a lender's share of a borrower's emissions, calculated as Lender Exposure divided by the Denominator (Total Debt, EVIC, or Property Value).
Enterprise Value Including Cash. The total market value of a company including equity and debt. Used as the denominator for listed equity and corporate bond positions.
The sum of all outstanding debt obligations of a borrower. Used as the denominator for loan exposures.
The market or appraised value of a property. Used as the denominator for real estate loans and mortgages.
Total government revenue for the reporting year. Used as the sovereign denominator for government bond positions.
Data Quality Score. PCAF's 1-5 scoring system for the quality of emission data used. DQS 1 = highest quality. DQS 5 = lowest quality.
Counterparty has provided a GHG inventory report. DQS 1 = third-party verified. DQS 2 = self-reported.
Counterparty has provided fuel or electricity consumption data. Scope 1 and Scope 2 estimated using IPCC emission factors.
Counterparty has provided revenue and sector data. Emissions estimated using MRIO sector intensity factors from EORA26.
No data available from counterparty. Emissions estimated using a range based on portfolio average intensity and loan size.
Special methodology for government bond counterparties. Uses national government consumption emissions scaled by lender share of government revenue or GDP.
Multi-Region Input-Output tables. Economic models that estimate emission intensity by sector and country. Used for Option 3 estimation.
Simplified version of the EORA MRIO database covering 190 countries and 26 harmonised sectors.
International Standard Industrial Classification. The UN's system for classifying economic activities by sector code.
A scalar applied to MRIO base intensities to account for Nigeria's higher emission intensity relative to global averages, driven by generator dependence and grid unreliability.
A coefficient that converts an activity into an equivalent quantity of GHG emissions.
Tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent. The standard unit for measuring GHG emissions, converting all greenhouse gases to their CO2 warming equivalence.
The Greenhouse Gas Protocol. The most widely used international accounting standard for measuring and managing greenhouse gas emissions.
USD/NGN exchange rate used to convert Naira figures to USD. Current assumption: 1580 NGN per $1 USD.
